The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun'''|上塩冶築山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-cho, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924, with the area under protection expanded in 2020.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191542 |title=上塩冶築山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster. The artifacts excavated from this site were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property}} in 2018.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/375887 |title=島根県上塩冶築山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located on slightly elevated ground on the north bank of the Kobe River in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled by the local landholder, a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered in 1887 and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were unearthed. Modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted since 1985. The tumulus is a circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of about 46 meters and a height of about six meters, (although the British archaeologist {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland}} recorded it as being a keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} in 1907). Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found on the surface and surroundings. A moat (approximately 16 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus.
The burial facility is a large horizontal cut stone chamber, containing two horizontal house-shaped stone sarcophagi, one large and one small. The burial chamber is orientated to the west-southwest and has a total length (including corridor) of 14.6 meters. This is the longest in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}}. The chamber itself has a length of 6.6 meters, width of 2.8 meters at the back wall and height of 2.9 meters. It is constructed with cut tuff stone, with one inner wall and four-tiered side walls. Ceiling stones and lining stones are natural stones, and river stones are laid on the floor. The side walls of the corridor are three-tiered cut stone, and the ceiling stone is natural stone. Many grave goods in good preservation were found, including jade beads, a gilt-bronze crown and iron sword and daggers from the small coffin, with a horse harness placed on the lid, and a round-pommel iron sword, iron spears, iron arrowheads, silver rings and other items from the larger coffin. Many of these items were retained by the local landowner, and were acquired by Izumo city in 1959. They are now kept at the Izumo Yayoi no Mori Museum.
From these artifacts, the Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period around the end of the 6th century<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[Nishidani Tombs]] and the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] are located nearby. The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Larger Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sekkan-2-1.jpg|Smaller Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Burial Chamber looking towards entrance 上塩冶築山古墳出土 金銀装円頭大刀-2.JPG|Iron Sword recovered from tomb (ICP) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶築山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.35|N|132.760769|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|21|0.00|N|132|45|38.40|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun'''|上塩冶築山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-cho, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924, with the area under protection expanded in 2020.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191542 |title=上塩冶築山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster. The artifacts excavated from this site were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2018.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/375887 |title=島根県上塩冶築山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located on slightly elevated ground on the north bank of the Kobe River in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled by the local landholder, a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered in 1887 and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were unearthed. Modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted since 1985. The tumulus is a circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of about 46 meters and a height of about six meters, (although the British archaeologist {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland|1=William Gowland}} recorded it as being a keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} in 1907). Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found on the surface and surroundings. A moat (approximately 16 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus.
The burial facility is a large horizontal cut stone chamber, containing two horizontal house-shaped stone sarcophagi, one large and one small. The burial chamber is orientated to the west-southwest and has a total length (including corridor) of 14.6 meters. This is the longest in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}}. The chamber itself has a length of 6.6 meters, width of 2.8 meters at the back wall and height of 2.9 meters. It is constructed with cut tuff stone, with one inner wall and four-tiered side walls. Ceiling stones and lining stones are natural stones, and river stones are laid on the floor. The side walls of the corridor are three-tiered cut stone, and the ceiling stone is natural stone. Many grave goods in good preservation were found, including jade beads, a gilt-bronze crown and iron sword and daggers from the small coffin, with a horse harness placed on the lid, and a round-pommel iron sword, iron spears, iron arrowheads, silver rings and other items from the larger coffin. Many of these items were retained by the local landowner, and were acquired by Izumo city in 1959. They are now kept at the Izumo Yayoi no Mori Museum.
From these artifacts, the Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period around the end of the 6th century<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[Nishidani Tombs]] and the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] are located nearby. The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Larger Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sekkan-2-1.jpg|Smaller Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Burial Chamber looking towards entrance 上塩冶築山古墳出土 金銀装円頭大刀-2.JPG|Iron Sword recovered from tomb (ICP) </gallery>

From these artifacts, the Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period around the end of the 6th century<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[Nishidani Tombs]] and the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] are located nearby. The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Larger Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sekkan-2-1.jpg|Smaller Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Burial Chamber looking towards entrance 上塩冶築山古墳出土 金銀装円頭大刀-2.JPG|Iron Sword recovered from tomb (ICP) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[en:Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]][[ja:上塩冶築山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[en:Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]][[ja:上塩冶築山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[en:Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]][[ja:上塩冶築山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[en:Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]][[ja:上塩冶築山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶築山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.35|N|132.760769|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|21|0.00|N|132|45|38.40|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun'''|上塩冶築山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-cho, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924, with the area under protection expanded in 2020.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191542 |title=上塩冶築山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster. The artifacts excavated from this site were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2018.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/375887 |title=島根県上塩冶築山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located on slightly elevated ground on the north bank of the Kobe River in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled by the local landholder, a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered in 1887 and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were unearthed. Modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted since 1985. The tumulus is a circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of about 46 meters and a height of about six meters, (although the British archaeologist {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland|1=William Gowland}} recorded it as being a keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} in 1907). Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found on the surface and surroundings. A moat (approximately 16 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus.
The burial facility is a large horizontal cut stone chamber, containing two horizontal house-shaped stone sarcophagi, one large and one small. The burial chamber is orientated to the west-southwest and has a total length (including corridor) of 14.6 meters. This is the longest in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}}. The chamber itself has a length of 6.6 meters, width of 2.8 meters at the back wall and height of 2.9 meters. It is constructed with cut tuff stone, with one inner wall and four-tiered side walls. Ceiling stones and lining stones are natural stones, and river stones are laid on the floor. The side walls of the corridor are three-tiered cut stone, and the ceiling stone is natural stone. Many grave goods in good preservation were found, including jade beads, a gilt-bronze crown and iron sword and daggers from the small coffin, with a horse harness placed on the lid, and a round-pommel iron sword, iron spears, iron arrowheads, silver rings and other items from the larger coffin. Many of these items were retained by the local landowner, and were acquired by Izumo city in 1959. They are now kept at the Izumo Yayoi no Mori Museum.
From these artifacts, the Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period around the end of the 6th century<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[Nishidani Tombs]] and the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] are located nearby. The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Larger Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sekkan-2-1.jpg|Smaller Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Burial Chamber looking towards entrance 上塩冶築山古墳出土 金銀装円頭大刀-2.JPG|Iron Sword recovered from tomb (ICP) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

==External links== {{Commonscat|Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun}}

Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled by the local landholder, a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered in 1887 and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were unearthed. Modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted since 1985. The tumulus is a circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of about 46 meters and a height of about six meters, (although the British archaeologist {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|qid=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland|1=William Gowland}} recorded it as being a keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} in 1907). Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found on the surface and surroundings. A moat (approximately 16 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus.
The burial facility is a large horizontal cut stone chamber, containing two horizontal house-shaped stone sarcophagi, one large and one small. The burial chamber is orientated to the west-southwest and has a total length (including corridor) of 14.6 meters. This is the longest in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}}. The chamber itself has a length of 6.6 meters, width of 2.8 meters at the back wall and height of 2.9 meters. It is constructed with cut tuff stone, with one inner wall and four-tiered side walls. Ceiling stones and lining stones are natural stones, and river stones are laid on the floor. The side walls of the corridor are three-tiered cut stone, and the ceiling stone is natural stone. Many grave goods in good preservation were found, including jade beads, a gilt-bronze crown and iron sword and daggers from the small coffin, with a horse harness placed on the lid, and a round-pommel iron sword, iron spears, iron arrowheads, silver rings and other items from the larger coffin. Many of these items were retained by the local landowner, and were acquired by Izumo city in 1959. They are now kept at the Izumo Yayoi no Mori Museum.
From these artifacts, the Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period around the end of the 6th century<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[Nishidani Tombs]] and the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] are located nearby. The Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun is located 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|qid=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Larger Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sekkan-2-1.jpg|Smaller Sarcophagus Kamienya Tsukiyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Burial Chamber looking towards entrance 上塩冶築山古墳出土 金銀装円頭大刀-2.JPG|Iron Sword recovered from tomb (ICP) </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun'''|上塩冶築山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-cho, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|qid=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The artifacts excavated from this site were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2018.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/375887 |title=島根県上塩冶築山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
